strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React production experience across multiple companies (Optilyz, RIDE Capital, re:cinq), directly matching the primary stack
Explicit AI-agent infrastructure work at current role, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — rare and directly required
Strong TDD and testing culture: created two testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), organized Refactoring Dojos, delivered testing workshops, practiced trunk-based development
concerns: NestJS is absent from the resume — it is listed as a primary backend framework and there is no evidence of experience with it
Kotlin is not mentioned at all; Java appears only briefly (robotic arms at Altom, 2016–2018) with no backend service context
Named testing frameworks (Vitest, Playwright, Jest) are not cited by name — testing experience is evident but alignment to the specific toolchain is unconfirmed
response.justification: Bogdan has 15+ years of professional software development, with consistent TypeScript, Node.js, and React usage across his last three roles — a direct match to the primary stack. His current position at re:cinq explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, satisfying the increasingly critical AI agent requirement. His testing discipline is unusually strong: he built open-source testing frameworks and delivered multi-day TDD courses, which aligns well with the quality and code-review expectations of the role. The primary gap is NestJS, which is listed as a core backend framework; there is no mention of it or an equivalent opinionated Node.js framework (e.g., Fastify with DI, Express with structured architecture). Kotlin is also absent. These are learnable gaps for a developer of this caliber, but they are real. The score reflects an excellent profile against roughly 80% of the requirements with two concrete and testable gaps.
